title: xtra gooey argentinian dough bake
description: A buttery, gooey, romantic argentinian dish featuring pico de gallo sauce, wood-fired-bake, low-glycemic-friendly

ingredients.name: low-glycemic all-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: use a low-glycemic flour option like almond flour or coconut flour as a substitute

instructions: in a large mixing bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, and salt.
add eggs and mix until dough forms.
knead dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
cover dough with plastic wrap and let it rest for 10 minutes.
divide dough into 2 equal pieces and shape into balls.
roll out each ball into a circle that is 1/2 inch thick.
place one circle on the preheated wood-fired brick and bake for 2-3 minutes until it starts to puff up.
spread a spoonful of pico de gallo sauce on top of the cooked dough circle.
add sliced mango, diced avocado, and chopped cilantro on top of the sauce.
place the second dough circle on top of the filling and press the edges together to seal the dough bake.
use a knife to cut a few slits on top of the dough bake to allow steam to escape.
bake for an additional 2-3 minutes until the dough is golden brown.
remove from the wood-fired brick and let it cool for 2-3 minutes before slicing and serving.
